summary: GNU tar saves many files together into a single tape or disk archive, and can restore individual files from the archive.

options.name: catenate
options.description: Append tar files to an archive

options.name: create
options.description: Create a new archive

options.name: delete
options.description: Delete from the archive (not on mag tapes!)

options.name: diff
options.description: Find differences between archive and file system

options.name: append
options.description: Append files to the end of an archive

options.name: test-label
options.description: Test the archive volume label and exit

options.name: list
options.description: List the contents of an archive

options.name: update
options.description: Only append files newer than copy in archive

options.name: extract
options.description: Extract files from an archive

options.name: check-device
options.description: Check device numbers when creating incremental archives (default)

options.name: listed-incremental
options.description: Handle new GNU-format incremental backup

options.name: incremental
options.description: Handle old GNU-format incremental backup

options.name: hole-detection
options.description: Technique to detect holes

options.name: ignore-failed-read
options.description: Do not exit with nonzero on unreadable files

options.name: level
options.description: Dump level for created listed-incremental archive

options.name: no-check-device
options.description: Do not check device numbers when creating incremental archives

options.name: no-seek
options.description: Archive is not seekable

options.name: seek
options.description: Archive is seekable

options.name: occurrence
options.description: Process only the NUMBERth occurrence of each file in the archive

options.name: sparse-version
options.description: Set version of the sparse format to use (implies --sparse)

options.name: sparse
options.description: Handle sparse files efficiently

options.name: add-file
options.description: Add given FILE to the archive (useful if its name starts with a dash)

options.name: directory
options.description: Change to directory DIR

options.name: exclude
options.description: Exclude files, given as a PATTERN

options.name: exclude-backups
options.description: Exclude backup and lock files

options.name: exclude-caches
options.description: Exclude contents of directories containing CACHEDIR.TAG, except for the tag file itself

options.name: exclude-caches-all
options.description: Exclude directories containing CACHEDIR.TAG

options.name: exclude-caches-under
options.description: Exclude everything under directories containing CACHEDIR.TAG

options.name: exclude-ignore
options.description: Read exclude patterns for each directory from FILE, if it exists

options.name: exclude-ignore-recursive
options.description: Read exclude patterns for each directory and its subdirectories from FILE, if it exists

options.name: exclude-tag
options.description: Exclude contents of directories containing FILE, except for FILE itself

options.name: exclude-tag-all
options.description: Exclude directories containing FILE

options.name: exclude-tag-under
options.description: Exclude everything under directories containing FILE

options.name: exclude-vcs
options.description: Exclude version control system directories

options.name: exclude-vcs-ignores
options.description: Read exclude patterns from the VCS ignore files

options.name: no-null
options.description: Disable the effect of the previous --null option

options.name: no-recursion
options.description: Avoid descending automatically in directories

options.name: no-unquote
options.description: Do not unquote input file or member names

options.name: no-verbatim-files-from
options.description: -T treats file names starting with dash as options (default)

options.name: null
options.description: -T reads null-terminated names; implies --verbatim-files-from

options.name: recursion
options.description: Recurse into directories (default)

options.name: files-from
options.description: Get names to extract or create from FILE

options.name: unquote
options.description: Unquote input file or member names (default)

options.name: verbatim-files-from
options.description: -T reads file names verbatim (no escape or option handling)

options.name: exclude-from
options.description: Exclude patterns listed in FILE

options.name: anchored
options.description: Patterns match file name start

options.name: ignore-case
options.description: Ignore case

options.name: no-anchored
options.description: Patterns match after any '/' (default for exclusion)

options.name: no-ignore-case
options.description: Case sensitive matching (default)

options.name: no-wildcards
options.description: Verbatim string matching

options.name: no-wildcards-match-slash
options.description: Wildcards do not match '/'

options.name: wildcards
options.description: Use wildcards (default for exclusion)

options.name: wildcards-match-slash
options.description: Wildcards match '/' (default for exclusion)

options.name: keep-directory-symlink
options.description: Preserve existing symlinks to directories when extracting

options.name: keep-newer-files
options.description: Don't replace existing files that are newer than their archive copies

options.name: keep-old-files
options.description: Don't replace existing files when extracting, treat them as errors

options.name: no-overwrite-dir
options.description: Preserve metadata of existing directories

options.name: one-top-level
options.description: Create a subdirectory to avoid having loose files extracted

options.name: overwrite
options.description: Overwrite existing files when extracting

options.name: overwrite-dir
options.description: Overwrite metadata of existing directories when extracting (default)

options.name: recursive-unlink
options.description: Empty hierarchies prior to extracting directory

options.name: remove-files
options.description: Remove files after adding them to the archive

options.name: skip-old-files
options.description: Don't replace existing files when extracting, silently skip over them

options.name: unlink-first
options.description: Remove each file prior to extracting over it

options.name: verify
options.description: Attempt to verify the archive after writing it

options.name: ignore-command-error
options.description: Ignore exit codes of children

options.name: no-ignore-command-error
options.description: Treat non-zero exit codes of children as error

options.name: to-stdout
options.description: Extract files to standard output

options.name: to-command
options.description: Pipe extracted files to another program

options.name: atime-preserve
options.description: Preserve access times on dumped files

options.name: clamp-mtime
options.description: Only set time when the file is more recent than what was given with --mtime

options.name: delay-directory-restore
options.description: Delay setting modification times and permissions of extracted directories until the end of extraction

options.name: group
options.description: Force NAME as group for added files

options.name: group-map
options.description: Use FILE to map file owner GIDs and names

options.name: mode
options.description: Force (symbolic) mode CHANGES for added files

options.name: mtime
options.description: Set mtime for added files from DATE-OR-FILE

options.name: touch
options.description: Don't extract file modified time

options.name: no-delay-directory-restore
options.description: Cancel the effect of --delay-directory-restore option

options.name: no-same-owner
options.description: Extract files as yourself (default for ordinary users)

options.name: no-same-permissions
options.description: Apply the user's umask when extracting permissions from the archive (default for ordinary users)

options.name: numeric-owner
options.description: Always use numbers for user/group names

options.name: owner
options.description: Force NAME as owner for added files

options.name: owner-map
options.description: Use FILE to map file owner UIDs and names

options.name: preserve-permissions
options.description: Extract information about file permissions (default for superuser)

options.name: same-owner
options.description: Try extracting files with the same ownership as exists in the archive (default for superuser)

options.name: sort
options.description: Directory sorting order

options.name: preserve-order
options.description: Member arguments are listed in the same order as the files in the archive

options.name: acls
options.description: Enable the POSIX ACLs support

options.name: no-acls
options.description: Disable the POSIX ACLs support

options.name: no-selinux
options.description: Disable the SELinux context support

options.name: no-xattrs
options.description: Disable extended attributes support

options.name: selinux
options.description: Enable the SELinux context support

options.name: xattrs
options.description: Enable extended attributes support

options.name: xattrs-exclude
options.description: Specify the exclude pattern for xattr keys

options.name: xattrs-include
options.description: Specify the include pattern for xattr keys

options.name: force-local
options.description: Archive file is local even if it has a colon

options.name: file
options.description: Use archive file or device ARCHIVE

options.name: info-script
options.description: Run script at end of each tape (implies -M)

options.name: tape-length
options.description: Change tape after writing NUMBER x 1024 bytes

options.name: multi-volume
options.description: Create/list/extract multi-volume archive

options.name: rmt-command
options.description: Use given rmt COMMAND instead of rmt

options.name: rsh-command
options.description: Use remote COMMAND instead of rsh

options.name: volno-file
options.description: Use/update the volume number in FILE

options.name: blocking-factor
options.description: BLOCKS x 512 bytes per record

options.name: read-full-records
options.description: Reblock as we read (for 4.2BSD pipes)

options.name: ignore-zeros
options.description: Ignore zeroed blocks in archive (means EOF)

options.name: record-size
options.description: NUMBER of bytes per record, multiple of 512

options.name: format
options.description: Create archive of the given format

options.name: old-archive
options.description: Same as --format=v7

options.name: pax-option
options.description: Control pax keywords

options.name: posix
options.description: Same as --format=posix

options.name: label
options.description: Create archive with volume name TEXT; at list/extract time, use TEXT as a globbing pattern for volume name

options.name: auto-compress
options.description: Use archive suffix to determine the compression program

options.name: use-compress-program
options.description: Filter through PROG (must accept -d)

options.name: bzip2
options.description: Filter the archive through bzip2

options.name: xz
options.description: Filter the archive through xz

options.name: lzip
options.description: Filter the archive through lzip

options.name: lzma
options.description: Filter the archive through lzma

options.name: lzop
options.description: Filter the archive through lzop

options.name: no-auto-compress
options.description: Do not use archive suffix to determine the compression program

options.name: zstd
options.description: Filter the archive through zstd

options.name: gzip
options.description: Filter the archive through gzip

options.name: compress
options.description: Filter the archive through compress

options.name: backup
options.description: Backup before removal, choose version CONTROL

options.name: hard-dereference
options.description: Follow hard links; archive and dump the files they refer to

options.name: dereference
options.description: Follow symlinks; archive and dump the files they point to

options.name: starting-file
options.description: Begin at member MEMBER-NAME when reading the archive

options.name: newer-mtime
options.description: Compare date and time when data changed only

options.name: newer
options.description: Only store files newer than DATE-OR-FILE

options.name: one-file-system
options.description: Stay in local file system when creating archive

options.name: absolute-names
options.description: Don't strip leading '/'s from file names

options.name: suffix
options.description: Backup before removal, override usual suffix

options.name: strip-components
options.description: Strip NUMBER leading components from file names on extraction

options.name: transform
options.description: Use sed replace EXPRESSION to transform file names

options.name: checkpoint
options.description: Display progress messages every NUMBERth record (default 10)

options.name: checkpoint-action
options.description: Execute ACTION on each checkpoint

options.name: full-time
options.description: Print file time to its full resolution

options.name: index-file
options.description: Send verbose output to FILE

options.name: check-links
options.description: Print a message if not all links are dumped

options.name: no-quote-chars
options.description: Disable quoting for characters from STRING

options.name: quote-chars
options.description: Additionally quote characters from STRING

options.name: quoting-style
options.description: Set name quoting style

options.name: block-number
options.description: Show block number within archive with each message

options.name: show-defaults
options.description: Show tar defaults

options.name: show-omitted-dirs
options.description: When listing or extracting, list each directory that does not match search criteria

options.name: show-snapshot-field-ranges
options.description: Show valid ranges for snapshot-file fields

options.name: show-transformed-names
options.description: Show file or archive names after transformation

options.name: totals
options.description: Print total bytes after processing the archive

options.name: utc
options.description: Print file modification times in UTC

options.name: verbose
options.description: Verbosely list files processed

options.name: warning
options.description: Warning control

options.name: interactive
options.description: Ask for confirmation for every action

options.name: compat-o
options.description: When creating, same as --old-archive; when extracting, same as --no-same-owner

options.name: help
options.description: Give this help list

options.name: restrict
options.description: Disable use of some potentially harmful options

options.name: usage
options.description: Give a short usage message

options.name: version
options.description: Print program version

positionals.name: FILE
positionals.description: Files to add to or extract from the archive
